reforms.description: The government has implemented a renovation loan scheme in partnership with the European Investment Bank, offering loans at preferential terms to homeowners in multi-apartment buildings that commit to energy-saving measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This scheme promotes energy efficiency improvements, which can lead to lower energy costs and increased sustainability, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The volume of renovations has increased in recent years due to the scheme.
reforms.importance_rationale: The renovation loan scheme represents a significant step towards improving energy efficiency in residential housing, which is crucial for reducing energy intensity and greenhouse gas emissions.
reforms.source_quote: A renovation loan scheme has been established in partnership with the European Investment Bank, whereby loans are offered at preferential terms to homeowners in multi-apartment buildings that commit to energy-saving measures.

reforms.description: The Lithuanian government has established an energy efficiency fund of 80 million euros to improve the energy efficiency of public buildings and street lighting, with plans for additional financing from private sources.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving energy efficiency can lead to cost savings and enhanced productivity in public services, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to improve the energy efficiency of public buildings and street lighting.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a dedicated fund for energy efficiency represents a significant step towards modernizing public infrastructure, with potential long-term benefits for sustainability and economic performance.
reforms.source_quote: The government has committed to improve the energy efficiency of public buildings and street lighting. An energy efficiency fund of 80 million euros has been established for this purpose.

reforms.description: The Lithuanian government has prioritized reforming the vocational education and training system to better align it with the needs of the labor market, emphasizing the importance of work-based training.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing vocational education and training can improve the skill set of the workforce, addressing labor shortages and boosting productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Lithuanian government has prioritized reform of the vocational education and training system.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses critical skills gaps in the labor market and aims to enhance the employability of graduates, which is essential for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The Lithuanian government has prioritized reform of the vocational education and training system, which is largely school-based.

reforms.description: The government is promoting the development of Lithuania's venture capital market by establishing new venture capital funds and investing in existing ones, exemplified by the Baltic Innovation Fund, which finances high potential firms in the Baltic countries.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances access to finance for innovative firms, fostering entrepreneurship and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is actively promoting the development of the venture capital market.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of new venture capital funds represents a significant step towards improving the financing landscape for startups, which is crucial for innovation and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government is promoting its development by establishing new venture capital funds and investing in existing ones.

reforms.description: Lithuania has implemented reforms to product market regulations, significantly reducing the time and cost required to start a business, which has encouraged the entry of new firms and supported the growth of small businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering barriers to entry, these reforms enhance competition and innovation, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The number of days to register a business has fallen from 26 in 2005 to 3.5 in 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the business environment, facilitating entrepreneurship and innovation, which are critical for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The time to start a business was significantly reduced between 2003 and 2015, by which time Lithuania was ranked 8 of 189 countries for ease of starting a firm.

reforms.description: Lithuania has implemented reforms to simplify bankruptcy procedures, including reducing the timeframe for decisions on appeal and simplifying insolvency laws, although challenges remain.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying bankruptcy procedures encourages entrepreneurship and facilitates the reallocation of resources to more productive firms, enhancing overall productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Ongoing reforms have been noted to simplify bankruptcy procedures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform addresses a significant barrier to entrepreneurship and resource reallocation, which is crucial for improving productivity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: In the past decade there have been ongoing reforms in Lithuania to simplify bankruptcy procedures.

reforms.description: The JEREMIE initiative has been established to create venture capital funds using EU structural funds, aimed at financing various stages of firm development in Lithuania.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing venture capital funding, the initiative supports the growth of high-potential firms, facilitating innovation and economic development.
reforms.status_evidence: There have been advances in establishing venture capital funds through the JEREMIE initiative.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant step towards developing a venture capital market in Lithuania, which is essential for supporting innovative startups.
reforms.source_quote: There have been advances, such as through the JEREMIE initiative, whereby EU structural funds have been used to establish five venture capital funds.

reforms.description: The Lithuanian government has prioritized reform of vocational education and training (VET) to improve the skills of the workforce, aiming to double the proportion of work-based learning and in-company training by 2020.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the skills of the workforce is expected to improve productivity and innovation capacity in firms.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is aiming to double the proportion of work-based learning by 2020.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the approach to vocational education, aiming to align training with industry needs and improve overall workforce skills.
reforms.source_quote: The government is aiming to double the proportion of work-based learning in higher education and in-company training/apprenticeships that VET institutions provide by 2020.

reforms.description: The Lithuanian government has set a national R&D target for 2020 at 1.9% of GDP, aiming for half to be contributed by the business sector, to enhance the country's research and development intensity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the business sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has set a national R&D target for 2020.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to increase R&D investment, which is crucial for enhancing innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The Lithuanian government has set a national R&D target for 2020 at 1.9% of GDP, aiming for half to be contributed by the business sector.

reforms.description: The Lithuanian government is developing demand-side measures to support innovation, including innovative pre-commercial public procurement to create a market for innovations addressing societal challenges.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering a market for innovative solutions, this measure is expected to stimulate private sector innovation and address public needs effectively.
reforms.status_evidence: Legislation providing guidelines for pre-commercial public procurement was adopted by the government in July 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform introduces a new approach to public procurement that can significantly enhance innovation by creating demand for innovative solutions.
reforms.source_quote: The government is also developing demand-side measures to support innovation, such as innovative pre-commercial public procurement.

reforms.description: Lithuania aims to promote new forms of business financing and ensure that innovation policies support young innovative firms, alongside reforming bankruptcy procedures to expedite business closures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting young innovative firms can enhance entrepreneurship and drive economic growth through increased innovation and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative includes measures to support young innovative firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant effort to enhance the startup ecosystem, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Promote new forms of business financing and ensure that innovation policies support young innovative firms.

reforms.description: Lithuania plans to improve awareness of R&D tax incentives to promote innovation and enhance firms' absorptive capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing awareness of R&D tax incentives can stimulate private sector investment in research and development, leading to higher innovation outputs.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative aims to promote awareness of R&D tax incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: While important for enhancing R&D investment, the campaign is a targeted awareness effort rather than a structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: Promote innovation and firm’s absorptive capacity by improving awareness of the existence of R&D tax incentives.
